paper_title: Inferred from content: Seafloor DEM Collection and Processing

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting multi-beam survey (MBS) data and digital bathymetric charts from various organizations (Japan Coast Guard - JCG, National Institute of AIST, JAMSTEC, Japan Hydrographic Association - JHA) and processing them to generate, reprocess, combine, and overlap Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) of varying resolutions to create a composite seafloor DEM.

steps.description: The author collected data for the seafloor DEM from several organizations and researchers. The Japan Coast Guard (JCG) conducted a multi-beam survey (MBS) along the shallow seafloor off the northwestern coast of Okinawa Island from 2008 to 2012 (Yasuhara 2013) (Fig. 1C).
steps.searchable_summary: Download multi-beam survey data from Japan Coast Guard (JCG) archive (2008-2012).

steps.description: Using these data from JCG... the author generated 1.44-s-mesh (approximately 44 m) data of seafloor DEMs from the JCG dataset...
steps.searchable_summary: Generate Digital Elevation Model (DEM) at 1.44-second mesh resolution from JCG MBS data.

steps.description: The National Institute of AIST also obtained the DEM of the deep seafloor by MBS around the middle part of the Nanseishoto Islands in 2012 (Sato et al. 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Download multi-beam survey data from National Institute of AIST (2012).

steps.description: Using these data from... AIST, the author generated... 0.65-s-mesh (approximately 20 m) from the AIST dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate Digital Elevation Model (DEM) at 0.65-second mesh resolution from AIST MBS data.

steps.description: JAMSTEC (2020) stored and disseminated information regarding rock and sediment core samples, as well as the results of the MBS on their website, "Data and Sample Research System for Whole Cruise Information in JAMSTEC (Darwin)". The author retrieved these data from the JAMSTEC website...
steps.searchable_summary: Download multi-beam survey data from JAMSTEC Darwin website.

steps.description: The author retrieved these data from the JAMSTEC website and generated 2.03-s-mesh (approximately 63 m) data of seafloor DEM.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate Digital Elevation Model (DEM) at 2.03-second mesh resolution from JAMSTEC MBS data.

steps.description: The author reprocessed the 2.08-s-mesh (approximately 64 m) DEM using digital bathymetric charts (M7020) with 1-2 m interval counters proposed by the Japan Hydrographic Association (JHA) following the methods of Goto (2013)...
steps.searchable_summary: Reprocess Digital Elevation Model (DEM) at 2.08-second mesh resolution using JHA digital bathymetric charts (M7020) and specified method (Goto 2013).

steps.description: ...and combined these DEMs with the 9.69-s-mesh (approximately 300 m) DEM (J-EGG500) published by the JCG.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine the reprocessed JHA 2.08-s-mesh DEM with the JCG 9.69-s-mesh (J-EGG500) DEM.

steps.description: DEMs were overlapped according to their decreasing resolutions (Fig. 1C).
steps.searchable_summary: Overlap all generated/processed DEMs (AIST 0.65s, JCG 1.44s, JAMSTEC 2.03s, Combined JHA/JCG lower-resolution) based on decreasing resolution to create a composite DEM.
